messages.app.name: NodeQL
messages.project.new.title: New Project
messages.runtime.noExecutable: No executable SQL chain found under EXECUTE QUERY.
messages.settings.title: Settings
messages.tutorial.title: Learn NodeQL
messages.tutorial.solveFirst: Solve the short exercise to continue.
messages.tutorial.visual.runHint: NodeQL compiles your connected blocks into SQL.
messages.tutorial.visual.ready: You are ready to build your first visual query.
messages.tutorial.step.1.title: Build SQL without losing sight of SQL
messages.tutorial.step.1.body: NodeQL combines visual blocks with real SQL output. You can learn query structure, experiment locally and inspect every generated statement.
messages.tutorial.step.2.title: Three areas, one workflow
messages.tutorial.step.2.body: Choose blocks on the left, assemble them in the center and inspect SQL plus database results on the right.
messages.tutorial.step.2.question: Where do you assemble a query?
messages.tutorial.step.3.title: Read the blocks from top to bottom
messages.tutorial.step.3.body: A query begins below EXECUTE QUERY. SELECT chooses data, FROM chooses its source and WHERE filters the rows.
messages.tutorial.step.3.question: Which block chooses the table?
messages.tutorial.step.4.title: Shapes reveal valid connections
messages.tutorial.step.4.body: Drag a block near a compatible connector. NodeQL highlights valid targets and preserves the logical SQL order.
messages.tutorial.step.4.question: What should you do when no connector is highlighted?
messages.tutorial.step.4.answer.3: Move it to a compatible position
messages.tutorial.step.5.title: See the SQL before it runs
messages.tutorial.step.5.body: Mount a local SQLite database, review the generated SQL and select Run SQL. Results and understandable errors appear in the lower output area.
messages.tutorial.step.5.question: Where can you verify the generated statement?
messages.tutorial.step.5.answer.2: In the SQL output on the right
messages.tutorial.step.6.title: Plugin shapes communicate their role
messages.tutorial.step.6.body: Statement plugins join a chain, value plugins fit into value positions and container plugins hold nested blocks. Their extension rail identifies them at a glance.
messages.tutorial.step.6.question: Which plugin shape represents a reusable value?
messages.tutorial.step.7.title: Start small and inspect often
messages.tutorial.step.7.body: Begin with SELECT and FROM, connect a database and expand the query one block at a time. You can reopen this tutorial anytime from the school icon or Settings.
messages.languages.title: Language Packs
messages.languages.notConfigured: Online language catalog is not configured for this build.
messages.plugins.title: NodeQL Plugins
messages.plugins.noneHint: Install a plugin.json manifest to add blocks.
messages.plugins.repository.hint: Add trusted community catalogs. Manifests are verified with SHA-256 before installation.
messages.workspace.deleteRoot.title: Delete root script?
messages.workspace.deleteRoot.message: This will remove the trigger and its attached chain.
